title: Kiss Kiss Bang Bangalore

jokes.text: I'm not sittin' up front with you guys. It's bad for your eyes. Suckers.
jokes.setup: Homer claims he's avoiding the front seats for health reasons
jokes.punchline: Reveals he just thinks he's outsmarting his coworkers
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - thinks he's clever while being obviously wrong. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Mo-vie! Mo-vie! Mo-vie! Mo-vie! Mo-vie!
jokes.setup: Burns tries to hold a solemn moment of silence for dead workers
jokes.punchline: Workers immediately chant for the movie, completely ignoring the supposed tragedy
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons dark comedy - workers are so disconnected they don't care about workplace deaths. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: The american worker: Proud, tough, hardworking. And tired! These jobs are killing us. Outsourcing, take them away!
jokes.setup: Typical patriotic description of American workers
jokes.punchline: Workers desperately begging to have their jobs taken away
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire on outsourcing - subverts expected patriotic worker narrative with dark economic reality. Good political commentary.

jokes.text: It's a miracle! They moved our factory to a third-world nation. Now I have more time to play the lottery. Ka-ching!
jokes.setup: Worker celebrates losing his job to outsourcing
jokes.punchline: He's thrilled because he can now focus on the lottery - an even worse economic choice
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire about American worker priorities and economic illiteracy. The 'Ka-ching!' sells the delusion.

jokes.text: No, no. Your jobs are safe. They'll just be done by someone else in another country.
jokes.setup: Workers ask if they're losing their jobs
jokes.punchline: Burns uses corporate doublespeak to say yes while claiming no
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate doublespeak - technically true but completely misleading. Burns at his most evil/absurd.

jokes.text: So, congratulations, whoever catches this bouquet!
jokes.setup: Burns announces one worker will keep their job
jokes.punchline: He decides it wedding bouquet-style, treating job security like a party game
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd way to decide employment fate. The visual of grown men scrambling for a bouquet is perfect.

jokes.text: Woo-hoo! I win! In your face, best friends!
jokes.setup: Homer catches the bouquet and keeps his job
jokes.punchline: He taunts his unemployed friends about winning the 'prize' of having to work
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer - too dim to realize he's the only one who has to keep working. Character-perfect obliviousness.

jokes.text: Luxury? This thermostat is just painted on.
jokes.setup: Homer claims they live in luxury
jokes.punchline: Marge points out their thermostat is literally fake - painted on the wall
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag that perfectly deflates Homer's delusion. The painted thermostat is hilariously specific poverty.

jokes.text: Dad, that's Mac tonight. / Yeah? Well, while I'm gone, you'll do what he says.
jokes.setup: Homer appears to hire a babysitter
jokes.punchline: It's revealed to be the McDonald's Mac Tonight mascot, and Homer treats it as a real person
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Homer logic - treating a corporate mascot as childcare. The commitment to the bit makes it work.

jokes.text: Richard Dean Anderson.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy dramatically announces the actor's name
jokes.punchline: The reverent delivery of a fairly mundane celebrity name
jokes.explanation: Comic Book Guy's over-the-top reverence for B-list TV actors. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You finally came to our convention! / No. I'm looking for the convention for my new show, Stargate: SG1.
jokes.setup: Fans are thrilled their hero showed up
jokes.punchline: He's actually there for a different show and doesn't want to be there
jokes.explanation: Classic convention cringe - the awkwardness of unwanted celebrity encounters. Well-executed setup.

jokes.text: That show was so stupid. 'Oh, I'm MacGyver. I can make a bomb out of a banana peel and a toaster.' That show was just a paycheck to me, and nothing more.
jokes.setup: Actor appears at fan convention
jokes.punchline: He completely trashes his most famous show in front of devoted fans
jokes.explanation: Brutal meta-humor about actors hating their famous roles. The specific MacGyver parody is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Richard Dean Anderson just pissed off the wrong Richard Dean Anderson fans!
jokes.setup: Actor insults the show
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's overly dramatic threat using the actor's full name
jokes.explanation: Comic Book Guy's pompous outrage is character-perfect. The formal name usage makes it funnier.

jokes.text: How could he say that? Macgyver is my world. Richard Dean Anderson just pissed off the wrong Richard Dean Anderson fans!
jokes.setup: Fans devastated by RDA's dismissal of MacGyver
jokes.punchline: Threatens him with 'wrong fans' as if they're dangerous
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - treating MacGyver fans as threatening. Character appropriate for Patty/Selma obsessiveness.

jokes.text: Wait, wait. I have some even more exciting news! There is a girl in the audience! Everybody look for her!
jokes.setup: Announcement interrupts the search for Richard Dean Anderson
jokes.punchline: Finding a single woman at the convention is treated as more exciting than a celebrity
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about convention gender dynamics. The priority shift from celebrity to woman is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: This is a kilt, and I'm not a girl! / You're as close as we'll ever get! Get him!
jokes.setup: Willie protests he's not female
jokes.punchline: Desperate fans decide he's close enough and chase him anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of desperation. 'You're as close as we'll ever get!' is brutally honest and funny.

jokes.text: You can write 'help me' all you want. No one will ever see it.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy has kidnapped Richard Dean Anderson for autographs
jokes.punchline: He anticipates the actor will write desperate messages that fans will ignore
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about fan obsession. The preemptive dismissal of help messages is wickedly funny.

jokes.text: India air flight 57 now boarding first class, small children and fat guys. / That's me!
jokes.setup: Unusual airline boarding announcement includes 'fat guys'
jokes.punchline: Homer enthusiastically identifies himself in that category
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - zero shame about his weight, just excited for special treatment. Character-perfect.

jokes.text: I've never been less angry to receive a book.
jokes.setup: Marge gives Homer a management book
jokes.punchline: Homer's backhanded gratitude reveals his usual hatred of books
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer - even his gratitude reveals his anti-intellectual character. The phrasing is beautifully awkward.

jokes.text: You ignorant american, you have confused India with Indiana, Indiana with Illinois, and the Cubs with the Dodgers.
jokes.setup: Homer complains about missing American landmarks in India
jokes.punchline: Man catalogs Homer's multiple layers of geographical and sports confusion
jokes.explanation: Great escalating list of Homer's American ignorance. Each mistake gets progressively more ridiculous.

jokes.text: No! I took a job on the other side of the world!
jokes.setup: Homer realizing his mistake
jokes.punchline: Dramatic reaction to his own stupidity
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer realization moment. Predictable but fits character pattern of delayed understanding.

jokes.text: A cow took my iPod and I punched it!
jokes.setup: Homer calls home in distress
jokes.punchline: His emergency involves fighting a cow over an iPod
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Homer crisis. The image of him fighting a cow over technology is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Baby, did you lose weight?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to apologize to sacred cow
jokes.punchline: Uses typical romantic pickup line on a cow
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Homer applying human romance tactics to sacred cow. Absurd but character-consistent.

jokes.text: Come on, baby, don't play hard to milk.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to apologize to the cow
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun combining 'hard to get' with 'milk'
jokes.explanation: Classic dad joke territory. It works because Homer would definitely think this is clever.

jokes.text: I had to get out before another one of your bristly kisses.
jokes.setup: Anderson explains his escape from the fans
jokes.punchline: Reveals the kidnappers were forcing unwanted physical affection
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable fan behavior made explicit. The 'bristly' detail makes it more disturbing/funny.

jokes.text: I had to get out before another one of your bristly kisses.
jokes.setup: RDA explaining why he returned
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was being forcibly kissed by bristly-faced twins
jokes.explanation: Good cringe detail - reveals physical discomfort of kidnapping. 'Bristly' is perfect descriptor for the twins.

jokes.text: Then I remembered the blue contacts I wear to hide my latino heritage.
jokes.setup: RDA explaining his escape method
jokes.punchline: Reveals random fake biographical detail about hiding ethnicity
jokes.explanation: Absurd character detail that serves the escape plot. Random ethnicity revelation is weird but serves MacGyver parody.

jokes.text: Then I remembered the blue contacts I wear to hide my latino heritage.
jokes.setup: Anderson describes his escape plan
jokes.punchline: Random revelation about fake eye color and ethnicity
jokes.explanation: Weird non-sequitur that feels very Simpsons. Random celebrity 'fact' that serves the plot absurdly.

jokes.text: A-bra-cada-bra.
jokes.setup: Anderson uses a bra as part of his escape
jokes.punchline: Combines 'abracadabra' with the garment he's using
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay for the MacGyver escape sequence. The pun works with the visual gag perfectly.

jokes.text: And not just at 2:00 A.M. On the USA network.
jokes.setup: Anderson declares 'MacGyver lives'
jokes.punchline: Reference to when the show actually airs - late night reruns
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-joke about TV syndication. Self-aware humor about where old shows end up.

jokes.text: Well, is there anything in this bar that's made in America? / Just this. / God! Misfire!
jokes.setup: Customer asks what's made in America in the bar
jokes.punchline: Moe's gun is American-made, and it immediately misfires when shown
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy about American manufacturing quality. The immediate misfire is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Hindu. / What do you call the moisture on your hin? / Classic.
jokes.setup: Homer attempts to tell a joke about Hindu/moisture
jokes.punchline: Burns doesn't get it but Homer thinks he nailed it
jokes.explanation: Terrible pun that Homer loves - character-perfect. The 'Classic' self-assessment is the real joke.

jokes.text: In business as in breakfast, fried eggs have fragile yolks.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to sound managerial
jokes.punchline: Reads meaningless corporate wisdom from the book
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of corporate self-help nonsense. The egg metaphor is wonderfully meaningless but sounds profound.

jokes.text: Outsourcing.
jokes.setup: Bart calls with a bully problem
jokes.punchline: Homer applies outsourcing as solution to everything
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the episode theme. Homer's one-word solution to everything is perfect.

jokes.text: IBM help line, this is Brian. Have you tried disabling your firewall? / Dallas fort-worth marriott. How can I help y'all? Well, we have a cotton bowl special. Go aggies! / This is queen mama jumbo. The stars tell me you have a question. No, jenny, don't be dating that boy. The bones don't lie. He's bad mojo, girl.
jokes.setup: Man explains he works for several American companies
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire demonstration of multiple jobs with perfect American regionalisms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating sequence about call center outsourcing. Each persona is perfectly crafted with regional details.

jokes.text: Ibm help line, this is Brian. Have you tried disabling your firewall?
jokes.setup: Apu working multiple American service jobs
jokes.punchline: Generic tech support response with fake American name
jokes.explanation: Good satire of outsourced customer service. 'Brian' fake name and generic tech advice are accurate observations.

jokes.text: Dallas fort-worth marriott. How can I help y'all? Well, we have a cotton bowl special. Go aggies!
jokes.setup: Apu continuing service job montage
jokes.punchline: Adopts exaggerated Texas accent and sports enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good continuation - Apu mastering regional American dialects and cultural references for different service roles.

jokes.text: A nail file, a farmer's almanac, a gun with no bullets, some bullets and three of my MacGyver writers.
jokes.setup: Anderson plans another MacGyver escape
jokes.punchline: List gets increasingly un-MacGyver-like, ending with needing the actual writers
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-joke about TV writing. The escalation from MacGyver tools to literal writers is brilliant.

jokes.text: Having only one pancake leaves room for more bacon.
jokes.setup: Homer needs more corporate wisdom
jokes.punchline: Another meaningless breakfast metaphor that somehow works
jokes.explanation: Strong callback to the breakfast metaphor theme. Actually makes more sense than the first one, somehow.

jokes.text: I'm not a god. God has a white beard and invented the Da Vinci Code.
jokes.setup: Homer denies being a god
jokes.punchline: His definition of God includes writing a Dan Brown novel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer theological confusion. Mixing religious imagery with pop culture bestsellers is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Let's see... got the elephant man.. Johnny Six-Arms, papa Smurf.
jokes.setup: Worker explains Hindu polytheism
jokes.punchline: Homer's version includes the Elephant Man and Papa Smurf as gods
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer cultural misunderstanding. Mixing Hindu gods with pop culture figures is perfectly character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I won a free soda! I am a god!
jokes.setup: Homer questions if he's really a god
jokes.punchline: A vending machine prize confirms his divinity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - the smallest 'miracle' proves godhood. The economy and character integration are flawless.

jokes.text: I won a free soda! I am a god!
jokes.setup: Homer questions whether he might be a god
jokes.punchline: Winning a contest becomes divine proof
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - the smallest 'miracle' confirms his grandiose delusions. Great economy.

jokes.text: Those are corpses. / You never like my friends.
jokes.setup: Burns floating down Ganges with what he calls friends
jokes.punchline: Burns treats corpses as friends, Smithers as unsupportive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - treats death casually while focusing on social dynamics. Dark and quotable.

jokes.text: Both doors have tigers! / One of those tigers is named Homer simpson.
jokes.setup: Homer sets up a 'Lady or the Tiger' choice
jokes.punchline: Both doors have tigers, one just happens to be named Homer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the classic paradox. Homer's solution is both logical and completely insane.

jokes.text: We didn't have any ground beef. Yeah, but you did have slim jims, a cheese grater and rubber bands to hold it all together.
jokes.setup: Fans made MacGyver burgers
jokes.punchline: Applied MacGyver improvisation to cooking with disgusting results
jokes.explanation: Good callback that applies the MacGyver concept to food. The ingredients are appropriately gross.

jokes.text: We didn't have any ground beef. Yeah, but you did have slim jims, a cheese grater and rubber bands to hold it all together.
jokes.setup: Making MacGyver burgers
jokes.punchline: Using MacGyver-style improvisation for cooking
jokes.explanation: Good callback that applies MacGyver logic to mundane cooking. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: This is Selma in line at the luray caverns. It turned out it was the line for the ladies' room. This is the ladies' room.
jokes.setup: Showing vacation photos to RDA
jokes.punchline: Mundane mistake presented as vacation highlight
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - boring vacation photos with anticlimactic reveals. Shows twins' social awkwardness.

jokes.text: This olive garden coupon! It expires at midnight. / You're not holding a coupon. / MacGyver away!
jokes.setup: Anderson claims to have an escape tool
jokes.punchline: It's imaginary and he just runs away shouting his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - the 'escape' is just running away. The Olive Garden detail is perfectly mundane.

jokes.text: What did he write that in? / Grape juice and tears.
jokes.setup: Anderson left a message on the window
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's poetic description of the writing medium
jokes.explanation: Beautiful melancholy humor from Comic Book Guy. The combination of grape juice and tears is poetically pathetic.

jokes.text: You know, Smithers, 'I told you so' has a brother. His name is 'shut the hell up'!
jokes.setup: Smithers says 'I told you so'
jokes.punchline: Burns creates a family relationship between phrases to tell him to shut up
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Burns line - creative, character-perfect, and highly quotable. The metaphor structure is brilliant.

jokes.text: Yeah, what's mom's birthday? / It's... jan... feb... mar... it's may! May! Fir... secon... thir...
jokes.setup: Homer claims godlike knowledge
jokes.punchline: Can't remember basic family information, stumbles through calendar and numbers
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Homer's god complex. His struggle with basic information is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Overtime pay. Coffee breaks. Flex time. Casual fridays. On-site daycare. Low dental co-pays. Muffin baskets on your birthday. With mylar balloons.
jokes.setup: Workers explain what 'secrets' Homer taught them
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of basic worker benefits, ending with specific balloon detail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that satirizes how basic worker rights seem revolutionary. Mylar balloons is a perfect detail.

jokes.text: A-bom-shabai means 'vote union.' A-bom-shabai.
jokes.setup: Workers have been chanting meaningless phrase
jokes.punchline: Revealed to mean 'vote union' - Homer accidentally organized labor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to running gag. Homer accidentally creating a union while playing god is perfect irony.

jokes.text: Maybe you should take a personal day. Take one of mine. They are transferable.
jokes.setup: Worker suggests Burns take a personal day
jokes.punchline: Offers to transfer personal days like they're commodities
jokes.explanation: Good worker benefit humor. The casual generosity contrasts with American corporate culture.

jokes.text: But treating employees like human beings, that is madness!
jokes.setup: Burns was okay with beast combat but draws the line at worker benefits
jokes.punchline: Treating employees well is the real horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line that shows his twisted priorities. Great character consistency and dark humor.

jokes.text: How's chief Wiggum? / He was gravely wounded in a bank shootout. / Yeah, he's funny.
jokes.setup: Homer asks about Chief Wiggum
jokes.punchline: Lisa reports he was shot, Homer thinks it's comedy
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer humor - he's so disconnected he finds police shootings amusing. Character-perfect callousness.

jokes.text: You're the first man to ever outsource the american worker's sense of entitlement and privilege.
jokes.setup: Lisa praising Homer's accidental achievement
jokes.punchline: He exported American worker expectations rather than just jobs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation that ties together the episode's themes. Sharp social commentary with perfect phrasing.

jokes.text: Two months severance! Early retirement! Golden parachutes for all!
jokes.setup: Burns angrily fires everyone
jokes.punchline: His 'punishment' is incredibly generous benefits packages
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic escalation - Burns' anger results in workers getting everything they wanted.

tmdb_overview: At the power plant a movie is shown where the workers endorse outsourcing and Mr. Burns announces that he is closing the plant and moving the work to India. Homer is the only union employee left on the payroll and he is sent to India to train the replacement workers, when he succeeds beyond expectations, he is given total control of the plant and declares himself a god. Meanwhile, Selma & Patty take Bart and Lisa to their MacGyver convention. When they discover Richard Dean Anderson doesn’t like MacGyver and much as they do, they kidnap him in retaliation. Anderson escapes only to enjoy the thrill and he turns himself back in so that he can escape again. When it happens over and over again, soon Patty and Selma are the ones who need a means of escape.
